title: schoenus tricostularia
intro: Schoenus tricostularia, also known as the Finger Sedge, is a perennial plant in the family Cyperaceae, native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. Its health benefits include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and easing respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Schoenus tricostularia is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal use, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
